prior_auth_criteria: General Requirements for all medical necessity requests: (1) Diagnosis must be present; (2) Botulinum toxin administration no more frequent than every 12 weeks regardless of diagnosis; (3) Prescriber attests dosing is per FDA approved labeling OR for indications without FDA approved dosing, prescriber attests there is published clinical evidence to support dosing. Diagnosis-specific requirements also apply. Daxxify is typically excluded from coverage. Myobloc requires prior failure, contraindication, or intolerance to one of the preferred agents (Botox, Dysport, and/or Xeomin depending on indication) for indications including cervical dystonia, detrusor overactivity, sialorrhea, and spasticity. Botox for chronic migraine requires: diagnosis of chronic migraine defined by all of: >=15 headache days/month, >=8 migraine days/month, headaches lasting >=4 hours/day. Initial authorization up to 12 months; reauthorization up to 12 months.
covered_indications: Blepharospasm associated with dystonia (Dysport, Xeomin, Botox)
Cervical dystonia / spasmodic torticollis (Dysport, Daxxify, Xeomin, Botox, Myobloc)
Detrusor overactivity / detrusor hyperreflexia or detrusor-sphincter dyssynergia due to spinal cord injury or disease (Dysport, Botox, Myobloc with step therapy)
Hand dystonia - writer's, musician's, or typist's cramp (Dysport, Botox)
Hemifacial spasm / seventh cranial nerve disorders (Dysport, Botox)
Hyperhidrosis including gustatory sweating / Frey's syndrome (Dysport, Botox)
Sialorrhea (Dysport, Xeomin, Botox, Myobloc with step therapy)
Spasticity (Dysport, Xeomin, Botox, Myobloc with step therapy)
Migraine headache, chronic - prophylaxis (Botox only, with specific diagnostic criteria)
notes: Daxxify (daxibotulinumtoxinA-lanm) is typically excluded from coverage; coverage reviews may be in place if required by law or the benefit plan. Refer to Medical Benefit Drug Policy titled Medical Benefit Therapeutic Equivalent Medications - Excluded Drugs. Botulinum toxins are considered cosmetic when used to improve appearance or in absence of physiological functional impairment; most COCs and SPDs exclude cosmetic services. Many benefit documents also explicitly exclude treatment of hyperhidrosis; member-specific benefit plan document must be reviewed. Unproven indications (not covered) include: acquired nystagmus, anismus, benign prostatic hyperplasia, brachial plexus palsy, chronic daily headache, chronic low back pain, chronic prostatic pain, cricopharyngeal dysphagia (excluding achalasia), epiphora following salivary gland transplantation, esophageal spasm, gastroparesis, gustatory epiphora, lateral epicondylitis, lichen simplex, lower urinary tract dysfunction, motor tics, myofascial pain syndrome, nasal hypersecretion, pain/wound healing after hemorrhoidectomy, pancreas divisum, piriformis syndrome, post-parotidectomy sialoceles, post-thoracotomy pseudoangina, proctalgia fugax, severe bruxism, severe paradoxical vocal cord movement, sphincter of Oddi dysfunction, temporomandibular disorders, tension-type headache, thyroid associated ophthalmopathy, Tourette's syndrome, traumatic sixth nerve palsy, trigeminal neuralgia, trismus and stridor in ALS. Chronic migraine is unproven for Daxxify, Dysport, Myobloc, and Xeomin. Policy Number: 2026D0017AN.
quantity_limits: Botulinum toxin administration no more frequent than every 12 weeks, regardless of diagnosis.
Dosing must be per FDA approved labeling or supported by published clinical evidence.
Initial and reauthorization periods are no more than 12 months each for Myobloc indications.
